audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed as Job's answer to Eliphaz: friends as miserable comforters, destructive speech, God as attacker imagery, torn body, clean prayer claim, blood/earth cry, and witness in heaven language.

verses.restored: Job answered and said:
I have heard many such things; miserable comforters are all of you.
Is there an end to windy words? What provokes you that you answer?
I also could speak like you, if your soul were in place of my soul; I could join words against you and shake my head at you.
I would strengthen you with my mouth, and the moving of my lips would hold back your grief.
If I speak, my pain is not held back; if I cease, what departs from me?
Surely now he has worn me out; you have made desolate all my company.
You have shriveled me; it has become a witness. My leanness rises against me and answers to my face.
His anger has torn and opposed me; he gnashes his teeth at me. My adversary sharpens his eyes against me.
They have gaped at me with their mouth; with reproach they strike my cheek; together they gather against me.
The Divine hands me over to the unjust and throws me into the hands of the wicked.
I was at ease, and he shattered me; he seized me by the neck and dashed me to pieces. He set me up as his target.
His archers surround me; he pierces my kidneys and does not spare; he pours my gall on the ground.
He breaches me with breach upon breach; he runs against me like a warrior.
I have sewed sackcloth over my skin and thrust my horn into the dust.
My face is red from weeping, and deep shadow is on my eyelids,
though there is no violence in my hands, and my prayer is clean.
Earth, do not cover my blood, and let there be no resting place for my cry.
Even now, look, my witness is in the heavens, and my testifier is on high.
My friends are my mockers; my eye pours tears to the Divine,
that he would argue for a man with the Divine, as a son of humanity argues for his friend.
For a few years will come, and I will go the path from which I will not return.
